provenance.source_corpus: Large Woody Debris Structures and Their Influence on Atlantic Salmon Spawning in a Stream in Nova Scotia, Canada

nodes.name: artificial structures mimicking naturally fallen trees

source_spans.text: We installed artificial structures mimicking naturally fallen trees in a third-order stream in northern Nova Scotia from 1992 to 2004 to determine whether the structures enhanced spawning success of Atlantic salmon Salmo salar.
Spawning rates (redd counts) in the whole brook increased exponentially for the first 4 years after restoration work began, from 43 in 1992 to 592 in 1996.

edges.original_sentence: Artificial structures mimicking naturally fallen wood are effective in the restoration of spawning habitat.

source_spans.text: Artificial structures mimicking naturally fallen wood are effective in the restoration of spawning habitat.
